title: 48V Telecom Bus → 3.3V/10A
description: An isolated forward converter stepping down the standard −48 V telecom bus (represented here as a positive 44–52 V range) to a regulated 3.3 V/10 A output for network equipment line cards.
design_notes: The forward converter is preferred over the flyback at this power level (33 W) because it transfers energy directly through the transformer during the switch-on phase, reducing transformer core utilisation stress. The turns ratio is chosen so that the duty cycle lands near 0.35–0.45 at nominal input (48 V), leaving headroom for feed-forward regulation down to 44 V. The 3 % leakage ratio models a practical ungapped ETD core; the RCD clamp dissipates the leakage energy stored each cycle. Synchronous rectification on the secondary eliminates the Schottky diode drop, recovering roughly 3 % efficiency at 10 A. Careful gate-drive dead-time management is essential to prevent shoot-through in the synchronous rectifier.
source: Erickson & Maksimovic §6.3; TI SLUA107 — Designing a Forward Converter
